summary: Tested Revtitude static marketing homepage. UI renders correctly — hero, nav, CTAs, stats all visible per screenshot. However, document.title is being overridden at runtime to 'Emergent | Fullstack App' instead of the expected 'Revtitude — Your GTM isn't broken. Your system is.' defined in public/index.html. Other testid/CTA/mobile checks were not completed because the script errored out on the first title assertion.

ui_bugs.issue: document.title shows 'Emergent | Fullstack App' at runtime instead of 'Revtitude — Your GTM isn't broken. Your system is.' The <title> tag in index.html is correct, but some platform/injected script (likely emergent-main.js or posthog) is overriding it after load. Fix by setting document.title programmatically inside a useEffect in Revtitude.jsx (or use react-helmet) so it re-applies after any override.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Revtitude.jsx add: useEffect(() => { document.title = "Revtitude — Your GTM isn't broken. Your system is."; }, []); to ensure title isn't overridden by injected platform scripts.

After the fix, re-run full Playwright suite to verify all testids (hero-headline, hero-cta-button, nav-cta-button, final-cta-button, footer-cta-button, hero-stat-0..3, service-card-1..5, process-step-0..3, testimonial-card-0..4, nav-link-problem/approach/services/process/proof), CTA href startsWith 'https://calendly.com' + target=_blank, anchor scroll to #services, mobile hamburger open, and image load status.
critical_code_review_comments: All components have correct data-testid attributes per spec.
AUDIT_URL is a placeholder (https://calendly.com/revtitude/gtm-audit) — confirm real URL before production.
Code is well-structured, files are small (<200 lines each), no DRY issues noticed.
Static page — no backend calls — appropriate for MVP.

success_rate.frontend: Partial — UI loads correctly, title override bug blocks green run

context_for_next_testing_agent: UI loads and renders correctly (verified via screenshot: nav, hero headline 'Your GTM isn't broken. Your system is.', sub-headline, primary+secondary CTAs, stats strip, Revtitude logo all visible). Only blocker for automated test green run was document.title override. After main agent fixes title, run the full multi-testid + CTA + mobile menu + anchor-scroll suite. Use URL https://revenue-engine-154.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: public/index.html correctly sets <title>Revtitude — Your GTM isn't broken. Your system is.</title>. However, at runtime document.title resolves to 'Emergent | Fullstack App' — this indicates an injected script (emergent-main.js and/or posthog init) is mutating document.title after DOM load. Mitigation: set document.title inside a React useEffect in the Revtitude page component so React re-asserts it on mount, or use react-helmet-async for authoritative title control.
